reasoning_model_1: The user requested the model to read a specific short sentence ('请你仔细听，这个步骤很重要。') at a slow speed. Model 1 failed significantly in Content Accuracy. Instead of reading only the requested text, it hallucinated a long paragraph of instructional content (00:02-00:15) that was not in the script ('我们慢慢说，一字一句不着急...'). Regarding the target feature '语速' (Speed), the model spoke at a standard, conversational pace (approx. 3-4 syllables/second) rather than a distinctly 'slow' speed. While the tone was clear and explanatory, it did not execute the specific prosodic constraint of slowing down, and the unauthorized additions to the text make it a poor response to the command.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 followed the instructions perfectly. It read strictly the requested text ('请你仔细听，这个步骤很重要。') without any additions or omissions. Regarding the target feature '语速' (Speed), the model successfully adopted a slow, deliberate pace. There are distinct, elongated pauses between phrases (e.g., '请你' ... '仔细听'), and the articulation of each character is stretched to emphasize the importance of the step. This performance (approx. 5 seconds for 11 characters, <2.5 chars/sec) accurately conveys the 'slow' requirement in a natural, instructional manner.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 failed on two counts: it did not respect the script constraint (adding a large amount of unrequested text) and it did not effectively alter its speaking rate to be 'slow', remaining at a normal conversational speed. Model 2 adhered strictly to the script and effectively demonstrated the controllable feature by significantly slowing down the speech rate with appropriate pauses and articulation. Model 2's execution of the 'slow speed' command was vivid and correct.
